title: Fix mobile filename overflow and polish home page UI

requirements.text: Fix mobile responsiveness so long filenames never overflow outside the layout in all places filenames are displayed (selected file list on the home/create page, upload progress status card, and file list on the paste view page). Use responsive layout rules (e.g., allow wrapping/stacking on small screens, ensure flex children have min-width:0, and apply truncation or break-word behavior as appropriate) so the UI stays within the viewport on narrow devices.

quotes: file name is going outside on mobile devices. It's not responsive.

acceptanceCriteria: On ~360px-wide screens, long filenames remain fully contained within the card/list row without horizontal scrolling.
CreatePastePage selected-files rows remain readable on mobile (filename truncates or wraps) and action buttons (remove) remain reachable without pushing content outside the container.
UploadProgressStatus filename line does not expand the card beyond the viewport; the filename truncates cleanly on small screens.
PasteViewPage file rows remain within the viewport on mobile; if necessary, the Download button stacks below the filename on small screens while remaining aligned inline on larger screens.

requirements.text: Update the home page (CreatePastePage at route “/”) to look more professional through UI/UX polish only: improve visual hierarchy, spacing, typography, and section structure for the hero/intro and the create form while keeping the existing functionality and wording intent. Ensure the page feels cohesive in both light and dark mode.

quotes: Also work on home page to give it more professional look

acceptanceCriteria: Home/create page presents a clearer hierarchy (distinct hero/intro vs form area) with consistent spacing and alignment.
No functionality changes: users can still enter a message, upload files, select expiration, and create a paste as before.
Design remains consistent and polished in both light and dark themes (no low-contrast text, no clashing surfaces).

requirements.text: Apply a single coherent visual theme across the home/create page and paste view page to reinforce a professional look (e.g., a neutral, modern editorial style with subtle borders, consistent card elevation, and restrained accent usage). Enforce the theme consistently across key components without introducing new product features.

quotes: work on home page to give it more professional look

acceptanceCriteria: Cards, headings, descriptions, and action buttons follow a consistent styling approach (spacing, border treatment, and emphasis states).
No new feature behavior is introduced; changes are purely presentational.
The updated styling does not reduce usability on mobile (tap targets remain usable and content remains legible).

constraints: Frontend immutable paths must not be modified: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
Keep the backend (Motoko) logic and data model unchanged for this request.
nonGoals: Adding new backend endpoints or changing paste/file storage behavior.
Adding new authentication providers (Internet Identity remains the only auth).
Implementing real-time features (e.g., upload streaming via WebSockets) beyond current behavior.
